system_context.business_goals: To achieve a blend which isolates critical functionality, flexible control, and user support while meeting the needs of the broadest collection of serious users.

architectures.name: WinSAAM Production Migration Architecture
architectures.description: A hybrid architecture designed to bridge legacy numerical processing with modern graphical user interface requirements. It wraps the core SAAM/Consam mathematical engine in a Windows-native shell, utilizing DLLs to provide modularity for data entry, file management, and visualization.
architectures.architecture_characteristics: Modularity, Backward Compatibility, Extensibility, and Interoperability between legacy CLI logic and modern GUI components.

key_components.responsibilities: Processing heart of the system; performs system solving, numerical analysis, and iterative parameter adjustment.
Supervisory role; manages terminal input, CLI-style processing control, and coordinates other modules.
Assembles input components, edits project specifications, and manages project-level orchestration.
Handles tabular-oriented input, processing, output, and data formatting.
Coordinates user files, facilitates data access across modules, and ensures file integrity.
Responsible for plotting results, configuring visual outputs, and chart-based communication.
Line-oriented text editor for manual script editing and graphic text management.
Provides pre-defined kinetic models for specific application areas like lipid metabolism.

architectures.data_flow: User specifications are defined via the Editor or Spreadsheet and organized into projects by the Linker. The File Manager ensures data integrity before passing parameters to the SAAM Kernel. The Kernel executes mathematical modeling and returns raw data to the Spreadsheet for formatting and the Chart Manager for visualization.
architectural_constraints: Strict dependency on Windows OS environment
Reliance on Dynamic Linking Libraries (DLLs) for component communication
Maintenance of line-oriented text processing to support legacy Consam users
Kernel acts as a 'black box' for mathematical processing
architectures.notes: The architecture easily accommodates new domain-specific libraries and GUI improvements. However, it cannot easily accommodate cross-platform deployment (e.g., Linux/Web) or real-time collaborative modeling without significant changes to the File Manager and Kernel integration layers.

scenario_elicitation.elicitation_method: AI-Driven Brainstorming based on System Context

scenarios.scenario_text: A nutritional researcher performs a kinetic simulation by inputting data into the Spreadsheet and generating a visualization in the Chart Manager, with the SAAM/Consam Kernel completing the calculation in under 5 seconds.
scenarios.feasibility_note: Directly supported by the current hybrid architecture which bridges legacy kernels with modern UI components.
scenarios.stakeholder_rationale: Directly addresses the need for kinetic modeling tools for nutritional research in a Windows environment.

scenarios.scenario_text: A developer updates the numerical algorithms within the SAAM/Consam Kernel to improve precision, ensuring the Linker maintains compatibility without requiring changes to the Spreadsheet or Chart Manager UI components.
scenarios.feasibility_note: Supported by the modular DLL-based design, though the Linker's interface stability is a critical dependency.
scenarios.stakeholder_rationale: Isolates critical mathematical functionality from the user interface to ensure flexible control and long-term maintainability.

scenarios.scenario_text: A maintainer updates the Domain Libraries to include a new set of nutritional modeling templates and modifies the Editor to support new syntax, ensuring the File Manager can categorize these as new model types.
scenarios.feasibility_note: Indirect; requires the Editor and File Manager to be extensible enough to handle new metadata or syntax from updated libraries.
scenarios.stakeholder_rationale: Supports the 'broadest collection of serious users' by allowing the system to grow with evolving nutritional research standards.

scenarios.scenario_text: A system administrator deploys the WinSAAM package across a multi-user lab environment, ensuring the File Manager correctly handles user-specific configuration paths and the DLLs register without system-wide conflicts.
scenarios.feasibility_note: Directly supported by the Windows-native shell migration, though pathing logic must be verified for multi-user environments.
scenarios.stakeholder_rationale: Ensures the system meets the needs of a broad user base through stable deployment and user support structures.

scenarios.scenario_text: To support advanced research, the system is modified to allow the Chart Manager to export raw data directly to external third-party statistical software via the Linker or a new API bridge.
scenarios.feasibility_note: Requires exploration of the Linker's capability to expose data to external processes beyond the internal Windows shell.
scenarios.stakeholder_rationale: Explores 'flexible control' for power users who require integration with other serious research tools.

scenario_classification.rationale: The scenario describes the primary operational workflow of the architecture. The data flow from Spreadsheet to Kernel to Chart Manager is explicitly defined as a core capability.

The modular DLL-based design and the 'black box' nature of the Kernel specifically allow for internal algorithmic updates without necessitating changes to the UI components, provided the interface remains stable.

While Domain Libraries are extensible, modifying the Editor to support new syntax and the File Manager to recognize new model types requires logic changes to component internals rather than simple configuration.

The architecture notes indicate that multi-user environments and specific pathing logic for configurations are not natively optimized in the current File Manager and would require updates to handle per-user registry or directory isolation.

The current architecture is self-contained within the Windows shell. Exporting raw data to third-party software via an API bridge requires a new integration layer or significant modifications to the Linker and Chart Manager.

scenario_evaluations.change_description: None required; functionality is inherent to the production migration design.
None required; the DLL wrapper isolates the Kernel's numerical logic from the Spreadsheet and Chart Manager.
issues_identified: Potential regression if DLL export signatures are modified during precision updates.

scenario_evaluations.change_description: Update Editor (C-07) to include new syntax highlighting/parsing; Update File Manager (C-05) to include new file headers/metadata for categorization.
issues_identified: Legacy Consam users may find new syntax incompatible with older scripts if not handled via versioned templates.
scenario_evaluations.alternative_approaches: Use a sidecar configuration file to define new model types for the File Manager instead of hardcoding types into the component logic.

scenario_evaluations.change_description: Refactor File Manager to support environment variables (e.g., %APPDATA%) rather than static or relative paths to ensure multi-user isolation.
issues_identified: Legacy file access patterns might assume a single-user directory structure, leading to permission errors in locked-down lab environments.
scenario_evaluations.alternative_approaches: Implement a shim layer that redirects legacy file calls to user-specific virtualized folders.

scenario_evaluations.change_description: Develop a new API bridge or export DLL within the Chart Manager to format and stream data to external processes; modify Linker to handle external process handshakes.
issues_identified: The 'black box' Kernel might limit the exposure of intermediate calculation data needed by 3rd party tools.
Significant deviation from the current self-contained Windows shell constraint.
scenario_evaluations.alternative_approaches: Enhance the Spreadsheet component (C-04) to export standard CSV or Excel formats, which 3rd party tools can then ingest, avoiding a live API bridge.

scenario_interaction_weights.rationale: C-06 is central to Scenario S-05, which is classified as High Risk with widespread changes. The interaction between the Linker and external processes represents a significant architectural deviation. Failure to address this hotspot could lead to a failure in delivering 3rd-party integration, a key business requirement.

Similar to C-06, the Chart Manager (C-03) is hit by the high-effort S-05 scenario. It faces potential data exposure limitations from the 'black box' Kernel. If not prioritized, the architecture may fail to support the streaming data requirements necessary for external visualization tools.

The File Manager (C-05) carries operational risk regarding multi-user isolation (S-04). Failure to refactor for environment variables could lead to permission errors in locked-down lab environments, impacting the System Administrator's ability to deploy the software safely.

While C-01 is hit by two scenarios, both are classified as Direct with Low risk and Low effort. The architecture already inherently supports these scenarios via the existing production migration design and DLL wrapping logic.

architectural_strengths: Effective Core Isolation: The architecture demonstrates high confidence in maintaining core numerical logic, as seen in S-01 and S-02, where the DLL wrapper (C-01) successfully isolates the Kernel from UI components.
Seamless Production Migration: The inherent design of the production migration (S-01) requires zero changes, indicating the architecture is well-aligned with its primary deployment lifecycle.
Localized Modifiability for UI/Editor: Changes to the Editor (C-07) and syntax highlighting (S-03) are localized, suggesting a modular approach to user-interface extensions.
architectural_weaknesses: Rigid External Integration: The 'black box' Kernel and self-contained shell constraint create significant friction for 3rd-party tool integration (S-05), leading to high-risk, widespread change requirements in C-03 and C-06.

Legacy File System Constraints: The File Manager (C-05) relies on static path assumptions that conflict with modern multi-user isolation requirements (S-04), posing a risk to deployment in locked-down lab environments.
Data Exposure Bottlenecks: Current component coupling limits the ability to stream intermediate calculation data without significant refactoring of the Linker and Chart Manager.
overall_results.modifiability_assessment: The architecture is highly evolvable for internal core logic and localized UI updates but remains rigid and high-risk when adapting to external data interoperability or modern multi-user operating system standards.

recommendations.recommendation: Adopt a file-based exchange pattern (CSV/Excel) via the Spreadsheet component (C-04) for 3rd-party integration.
recommendations.rationale: Mitigates the high risk and widespread impact of creating a live API bridge for the 'black box' Kernel while still meeting the business goal of external tool support.

recommendations.recommendation: Refactor File Manager (C-05) to utilize environment variables and implement a virtualization shim for legacy paths.
recommendations.rationale: Addresses the medium-risk hotspot for multi-user isolation, ensuring the software can be deployed safely by System Administrators in lab environments.

recommendations.recommendation: Transition to a sidecar configuration model for file categorization and syntax definitions in C-05 and C-07.
recommendations.rationale: Prevents hardcoding of model types, allowing for more flexible control and easier support for future script types without component recompilation.

recommendations.recommendation: Establish strict versioning for DLL export signatures between the Kernel and the wrapper.
recommendations.rationale: Prevents potential regressions identified in S-02 during precision updates or numerical logic maintenance.
